decisions.decision: Individual Supabase accounts with email invite links
decisions.rationale: Enables per-member transaction attribution, RLS enforcement, and audit trails. Each household member gets their own login.

decisions.decision: Full common pool — all income is household income
decisions.rationale: Simplest model. All income flows into a shared pool, all expenses drawn from it. No personal allowance complexity.
Single-currency simplifies formatting, validation, and storage. No conversion logic needed.
Generous whitespace, one metric per card, calming colors. Friendlier for non-finance household members.

decisions.decision: All debts including informal (loans, credit cards, money owed to/from people)
decisions.rationale: Covers formal debts (bank loans, SACCO, credit cards) plus informal debts (money owed to/from friends/family). Reflects real Kenyan household finance patterns.

decisions.decision: Goal-based savings with target amounts and deadlines
decisions.rationale: Named goals (e.g., 'School fees - KES 200K by Jan') with progress tracking. More actionable than raw account balances.

decisions.decision: Fully custom — user defines all categories from scratch
decisions.rationale: No preset categories. Household creates their own category hierarchy. Maximum flexibility.
Auth, RLS, realtime, and Postgres in one service. Free tier sufficient for single household. RLS enforces data isolation.

decisions.decision: Manual entry + CSV import only (no bank API for MVP)
decisions.rationale: Zero cost, full privacy. Users enter transactions manually or import bank CSV exports. Plaid can be added later.

decisions.decision: Next.js App Router monolith with Server Components and Server Actions
decisions.rationale: All features as pages within one app. Server Components for data fetching, Server Actions for mutations. Simplest deployment (single Vercel project).

constraints: Single household only — no multi-tenant infrastructure
KES currency only — no multi-currency or conversion
Manual + CSV import only — no Plaid/bank API for MVP
Supabase free tier must be sufficient
All financial data behind Supabase RLS policies
No secrets or financial data in client-side bundles
openQuestions: CSV format mapping — which Kenyan banks' export formats to support first?
Notification strategy — email vs in-app for bill reminders?
Debt payoff visualization — simple progress bar or amortization schedule?

dataModel.notes: Single-entry bookkeeping for MVP. Debts table covers formal (loans, credit cards) and informal (person-to-person). Categories are fully user-defined with parent/child hierarchy.
